ops.title: Concurrent sessions on getaway can orphan in-flight work as an undescribed jj sibling change — recover with jj rebase, don't redo
ops.body:
The getaway repo is worked on by concurrent Claude Code sessions (cc-pool). Another session can land a commit on main mid-run (e.g. an automated guides re-render), which moves `@` to a fresh child of the new main and leaves the in-flight feature work as an undescribed **sibling** jj change — the working tree suddenly shows pre-feature files, but nothing is lost.

**Why:** jj snapshots the working copy into a change; `jj new main` elsewhere orphans it rather than deleting it.

**How to apply:** before diagnosing "my edits vanished", run `jj log` and look for an undescribed sibling holding the work. Recover with `jj rebase -r <change> -d main`, `jj new <change>`, then `jj abandon` any stray working-copy change (archive its diff first with `git diff HEAD > patch`). Agents editing files should verify their target content exists before applying review fixes — a blocked-with-findings return beats improvising against the wrong baseline.
